description_long: This is a large, heavy-duty hammer with a robust, polygonal head and a long, straight handle. The head is predominantly black, featuring angular, reinforced sides and a central striking face, suggesting it is designed for high-impact tasks. The handle appears to be made of dark wood or a wood-like material, with visible grain and a slightly worn texture, extending from the base of the head. The head has a unique, almost futuristic design, with a beveled edge and a possible metallic reinforcement on one side. There are no visible claws or peens, indicating this is likely a sledge or demolition hammer. The overall appearance is utilitarian and rugged, suitable for demanding urban construction or demolition work.
description: A large, black, polygonal-headed sledge hammer with a long, dark wooden handle and reinforced striking face, designed for heavy-duty urban tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the angular, polygonal head and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the broad, flat striking face and the symmetrical, reinforced head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the head's beveled edges and handle attachment.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the hammer head and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a dark finish, slightly textured and cylindrical, no visible ergonomic enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Polygonal sledge-type head with a broad, flat striking face and reinforced sides, no claws or peens.
